spec.description: Use-case-driven view of the ai-o11y-demo-apps demo, restyled to the Grafana AI Observability look (vivid KPI tiles, rainbow pixel bar, smooth gradient timeseries). Original 2026-05-23.

spec.title: Requests/hr by Model
spec.description: Weighted-pool model selection in action. Expect 6 Claude + 4 Ollama models to all be exercised over time (Opus tier is rare per the weights).

spec.title: Requests/hr by Agent
spec.description: Per-agent throughput. nc-chatbot/nc-gift-finder serve NeonCart shoppers. sb-router routes to sb-{billing, tech-support, account-management}.

spec.title: p95 Latency by Agent
spec.description: Tail latency per agent. SB router calls are usually fast (just routing), specialists (sb-billing etc.) are slower because they answer.

spec.title: p95 Latency by Model
spec.description: Compare model speed: small Ollama (qwen2.5:3b) fast, large Anthropic (claude-opus-*) slow. Useful for model-selection tradeoffs.

spec.title: Tokens/sec by Type
spec.description: Input vs output token rate. AI o11y story: most calls are input-heavy (system prompt + tool defs + history), with thin output. Watch input/output ratio.

spec.title: Tokens/hr by Model
spec.description: Token throughput by model. Lets you see which models are doing the heavy lifting (and consuming budget).

spec.title: Spend rate $/hr by Agent
spec.description: Per-agent spend rate. Helps spot agents that are burning budget disproportionately. Pair with the ROI panel below.

spec.title: Spend rate $/hr by Model
spec.description: Cost per model — Anthropic models cost ~10–100x more than Ollama (which is ~free per Stephen's local GPU). Watch the demo make this tradeoff visible.

spec.title: Total Requests (1h)
spec.description: Total LLM operation calls in the last hour, scoped to the selected agent/provider/model.

spec.title: Spend rate $/hr by Provider
spec.description: Provider-level $/hr. Lets a customer see exactly what each provider costs them over a rolling window.

spec.title: Top SB employees — calls / tokens / cost (24h)
spec.description: Highest-volume SupportBot users in the last hour. Pivot-table of per-employee call count, input tokens, output tokens, and cumulative cost. Acme employees (@acme.com) only — NeonCart shoppers are filtered out via gen_ai_agent_name=sb-*.

spec.title: Top 10 SB employees — calls/min
spec.description: Call rate for the busiest SupportBot users. Smoothed over a 5m window.

spec.title: Tokens/hour by SB employee (input + output)
spec.description: Token throughput per SupportBot user (per hour). Stacked area so it's clear who's contributing most to the team's token bill.

spec.title: Active SB employees (last 1h)
spec.description: Distinct Acme employees who chatted with SupportBot in the last hour.

spec.title: Avg calls per employee (1h)
spec.description: Mean call count across active employees. High number = power users; low number = spread across many people.

spec.title: Avg tokens per employee (1h)
spec.description: Average input+output tokens consumed per active employee in the last hour.

spec.title: Avg cost per employee (1h)
spec.description: Mean LLM spend per active employee. Useful for chargeback / capacity planning.

spec.title: Tool Calls/min by Agent
spec.description: Tool invocation rate per agent. nc-chatbot has search_products + navigate_to_search + add_to_cart. nc-gift-finder has search_by_criteria + add_to_cart. SB specialists have search_runbook + create_ticket. Sigil SDK doesn't attach gen_ai_tool_name to this metric, so it's per-agent only.

spec.title: ROI ratio per model (cart value / token cost) — 24h window
spec.description: The headline AI o11y story by LLM: did each model pay for itself in cart value generated? Above 1.0 = profitable; below 1.0 = bleeding. Scoped to NeonCart agents (nc-*) since SupportBot has no ATC events.

spec.title: Top Models by ROI Multiple — 24h
spec.description: Per-model ROI ratio over the last 24 hours: cart value attributed to the AI agent ÷ LLM token cost, grouped by gen_ai_request_model and ranked descending. Uses a fixed 24h window via increase()[24h] + instant — independent of the dashboard time range so the leaderboard is stable as you zoom around.

spec.title: Sessions starting per minute
spec.description: New shopping sessions/min broken down by user_domain (gmail.com / aol.com / yahoo.com / unknown for anonymous).

spec.title: Add-to-Cart by Source
spec.description: Manual vs ai_chatbot vs ai_gift_finder. The AI-source slices are what populate the ROI metric.

spec.title: Total Tokens (1h)
spec.description: Sum of input + output tokens consumed across all selected LLM calls.

spec.title: Revenue rate $/hr (completed orders)
spec.description: Realized revenue from /api/orders checkouts, projected to $/hr.

spec.title: Page Views (1h)
spec.description: Total NeonCart page views.

spec.title: Search Queries (1h)
spec.description: Total text searches against /api/search.

spec.title: Product Views (1h)
spec.description: Total single-product page hits.

spec.title: Transactions (1h)
spec.description: Completed checkouts.

spec.title: HTTP request rate by service
spec.description: OTel-autoinstrument http_server metrics — request rate per pod. Confirms every Python service is receiving traffic and exporting OTel metrics.

spec.title: HTTP p95 by service
spec.description: Service tail latency. Spikes here usually mean the service is queueing on a slow downstream (e.g. waiting on an LLM call).

options.content:
SIGIL · CONVERSATIONS
Open conversation viewer →
Every recent conversation across all agents, with model/cost/latency per turn.
SIGIL · ANALYTICS
Open analytics dashboard →
Plugin dashboard: eval scores, model breakdowns, conversation thread viewer.
USE CASES INSTRUMENTED IN THIS DEMO
Multi-model routing
6 Claude models (Haiku, Sonnet 4.5/4.6, Opus 4.1/4.6/4.7) + 4 Ollama models. Sticky per conversation.
Multi-provider routing
Anthropic cloud + Ollama local. Deterministic hash per session + conversation_id.
Tool use
nc-chatbot: search_products / navigate_to_search / add_to_cart. nc-gift-finder: search_by_criteria. sb-*: search_runbook / create_ticket.
Multi-turn conversations
Per-conversation history kept in memory (30m TTL, 20-turn cap) so follow-ups remember context.
AI ROI
neoncart_ai_attributed_revenue_usd_total tracks cart value generated by each AI agent vs LLM cost — the headline KPI.
The "mice" trap
nc-chatbot's search_products tool deliberately queries a non-existent species column for "mice", producing a real DB error in the trace cascade.
Conversion funnel
session_starts → page_views → search / product_views → add_to_cart → transactions → revenue. AI-vs-manual sliced via source.
Cost transparency
gen_ai_client_cost_usd_total emitted client-side by the gateway with full attribution (agent, model, provider, user_id).
Conversations are the 5th signal
Metrics, logs, traces, profiles — and conversations. Every conversation is a trace; not every trace is a conversation.

spec.title: Total Spend (1h)
spec.description: Cumulative LLM API spend in USD across all selected agents.

spec.title: p95 Latency
spec.description: 95th-percentile LLM call duration over the last 5 min.

spec.title: Error Rate %
spec.description: Percentage of LLM calls returning an error_type, over the last 5 min.

spec.title: Active Agents
spec.description: Distinct AI agent names that made at least one call in the last 5 min.

spec.title: Requests/hr by Provider
spec.description: Random-but-sticky routing — each conversation is hashed to one provider. Use this to confirm the gateway is exercising both Anthropic + Ollama.

spec.title: Token cost $/min (per model)
spec.description: Per-model token cost rate.

spec.title: Cart value $/min (per model)
spec.description: Per-model AI-attributed revenue rate.

options.content:
INTRODUCING
Grafana AI Observability
Actually useful AI O11y · use-case driven view of the ai-o11y-demo-apps demo
</> Instrument
Zero-config tracing
Drop-in SDKs for Go, Python, TypeScript, Java, and .NET. Capture every LLM call, token, and latency.
🔍 Explore
AI observability
Track latency, tokens, cost, and errors across providers and models. Follow conversations, agents, and generations in real time.
✓ Evaluate LEVEL UP
Automatic quality scoring
Use LLM judges, regex, JSON schema, or custom heuristics. Turn quality signals into dashboards and alerts.

spec.title: Grafana AI Observability — ai-o11y-demo-apps
